mnemonics: Ethereum is like a global computer that anyone can program (using Solidity) and pay for with Ether (gas). Think: World Computer, Gas, Solidity.
keyPoints: Ethereum is a programmable blockchain that allows for the creation of smart contracts and decentralized applications (dApps).
Ether (ETH) is the native cryptocurrency used to pay for transaction fees and computational services, known as 'gas'.
The Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM) is the runtime environment that executes smart contracts, ensuring they run exactly as programmed across all nodes.
commonMistakes: Confusing Ether (the currency) with gas (the unit that measures computational effort). Gas fees are paid in Ether.
Assuming all smart contracts are secure by default. They are only as secure as the code they are written with and are a common source of exploits.
Believing transactions are instant. They must be validated by the network and are subject to block times and gas prices, which can cause delays.
